candidates.name: Jared Polis
candidates.notes: First openly gay governor elected in US history. First Jewish governor of Colorado. Signed Reproductive Health Equity Act codifying abortion rights in state law (2022). Signed Amendment 79 implementing constitutional right to abortion and public funding for abortion care. Signed permit-to-purchase scheme for semi-automatic firearms (SB 25-003, 2025) — NRA called it 'Polis Permission Slip.' Signed ban on conversion therapy. Progressive on immigration — opposed border wall. Supports sanctuary policies. Signed sweeping gun control package. Heritage Action 3% lifetime. Despite progressive positions, has some libertarian economic instincts (reduced regulations, cut income taxes). Term-limited 2026.

profile.background: Tech entrepreneur and philanthropist; served on Colorado State Board of Education and as U.S. Representative (2009-2019); became 43rd governor in 2019 and first openly gay man elected governor in U.S.

candidates.name: Michael Bennet
candidates.notes: Senior senator from Colorado, now running for governor (announced April 2025). Born in New Delhi where his father served as US diplomat. Mother is a Holocaust survivor. Ran for president in 2020. Heritage Action 7% lifetime. NRA grade dropped from C+ (2010) to F. Supports assault weapons ban, universal background checks, red flag laws. Pro-choice (Planned Parenthood 100%). Supports comprehensive immigration reform with path to citizenship. Supports same-sex marriage. As Denver Public Schools superintendent, championed education reform including some charter school expansion — only conservative-adjacent position. Likely to resign Senate mid-term if elected governor.

profile.education: Wesleyan University (BA History), Yale Law School (JD)
profile.background: Former Denver Public Schools superintendent and chief of staff to Denver Mayor Hickenlooper. Worked at Anschutz Investment Company. Appointed to Senate in 2009.

candidates.name: John Hickenlooper
candidates.notes: Former brewpub owner turned mayor, governor, and senator. Quaker faith but does not emphasize religion publicly. As governor, signed Colorado's 2013 gun control package (universal background checks + high-capacity magazine ban) — cost two state legislators their seats in recall elections. Heritage Action 3% lifetime — 0% in both 117th and 119th sessions. Called for banning conversion therapy nationwide (Therapeutic Fraud Prevention Act). Supports comprehensive immigration reform. Pro-choice. Supports same-sex marriage. Strong on climate/environment (LCV 97%). Blocked Trump administration efforts in Senate. Running for re-election 2026. Former geologist — opposed fracking bans despite environmentalist base.

profile.education: Wesleyan University (BA English), Wesleyan University (MA Geology)
profile.background: Former Governor of Colorado and Mayor of Denver. Founded Wynkoop Brewing Company, one of first brewpubs in Colorado. Geologist turned restaurateur turned politician. 2020 presidential candidate.
